events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario is about gas piping with a missing sediment trap/drip leg. This is a code compliance issue under IFGC/NFPA 54.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 85,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Missing sediment trap/drip leg at gas appliance connection",
    "Debris or moisture in gas line can enter appliance without trap, creating ignition or clogging hazard"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install required sediment trap/drip leg downstream of appliance shutoff and as close as practical to appliance inlet",
    "Do not place system in service until trap is installed and leak-tested"
  ],
  "rationale": "IFGC/NFPA 54 require a sediment trap (drip leg) at each appliance to capture moisture and particulates before they enter the appliance. A missing trap is a non-compliant installation at rough-complete stage and poses a gas safety hazard.",
  "references": [
    "IFGC Section 408.4 Sediment Trap",
    "NFPA 54 (ANSI Z223.1) Section 7.7.1 Sediment Trap"
  ]
}
